content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 4, 1992

Ben Firschein  
Administrative Assistant  
California State Senate  
Tenth Senatorial District  
22634 Second Street, Suite 104  
Hayward, CA 94541

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-92-082

Dear Mr. Firschein:

This letter is in response to your request for confirmation and confirms the telephone advice provided to you on January 27, 1992, regarding the duties of the Honorable Bill Lockyer under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Under the provisions of the Act, do law books donated to a legislator's office constitute informational materials?

CONCLUSION

Law books donated to a legislator's office constitute informational materials and are not reportable gifts.

FACTS

Senator Lockyer, on whose behalf you seek our advice, is the Chairman of the Judiciary Committee of the California Senate. A publisher or an employee of a publisher proposes to donate law books to Senator Lockyer's office. The books will be used for official governmental functions and not for the private practice of law.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

ANALYSIS

As amended, the Act prohibits elected state officers from accepting gifts from any single source in any calendar year with a total value of more than $250. (Section 89504(a).) However, the Act specifically excludes from the definition of "gift" informational materials such as books, reports, pamphlets, calendars, or periodicals. (Section 82028(b)(1).) Accordingly, law books donated to a legislator's office are not gifts subject to limits when the books are to be used for official governmental functions and not for the private practice of law.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.2

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:bb

2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10¢ per page.
